key_insight: An AI-based ECG age gap can estimate “biological cardiac age” and identify people at higher cardiovascular risk beyond calendar age.
clinical_relevance: This non-invasive digital biomarker could help stratify cardiovascular risk in large populations and support earlier preventive attention.
method: Trained an AI-ECG model using the ECGFounder foundation model on 26,871 healthy UK Biobank ECGs.
Validated the model in 40,953 independent UK Biobank clinical ECGs with Cox regression for MACCE and related outcomes.
Externally tested performance in a hospital cohort (55,860) and a community cohort (27,065).
evidence_signal: The model showed moderate age prediction accuracy (r = 0.55; MAE = 5.12 years) and each 1-year increase in age gap was linked to a 13% higher MACCE risk in the clinical cohort.
watch_item: Because the biomarker was validated in cohort data, real-world adoption will require careful calibration, external generalizability checks, and prospective utility testing.
paper_title: AI-Derived ECG Age Gap as a Digital Biomarker for Cardiovascular Risk: External Validation in Hospital and Community-Based Prospective Cohorts
